retry_reason: Failure (return NULL) means the object's refcount was zero (object is dying/freed). Retrying cannot resurrect the object, so retry is pointless.
contract_summary: Conditional get-wrapper that acquires a reference on a bpf_crypto_ctx if and only if the object is still alive (refcount non-zero). Returns the same pointer on success, NULL on failure. The caller must check the return value and must later release the acquired reference via bpf_crypto_ctx_put (or similar) when done with the object. This function is typically called to prevent use-after-free when obtaining a reference to an existing object; it does not create a new object or allocate memory.

judge.reason: The function exclusively performs a conditional refcount increment (refcount_inc_not_zero) and returns the pointer (or NULL). No other logic is present, making it a pure get-wrapper.

check.reason: The refcount_inc_not_zero is called on &ctx->usage, incrementing the refcount of the object pointed to by ctx. On success, ctx is returned, so the get operation is on the return value. The access path ctx.usage.refs and type chain bpf_crypto_ctx--refcount_t--atomic_t are correct and consistent with the callee info.
